components.name: Cryptococcus 5-day spent minimal-media filtrate (25% v/v)
components.role: undefined complex supplement (thiamine bioassay / pantothenate source)
components.evidence: we monitored for thiamine while culturing Variovorax in minimal media with the addition of 25% spent media from a Cryptococcus five-day monoculture in minimal media ... quantified during the growth in 75% fresh minimal media and 25% filter-sterilized spent medium

components.name: (R)-pantothenate (vitamin B5)
components.evidence: we were able to detect ~5 mg/L of pantothenate in its spent media, suggesting that Cryptococcus production of pantothenate may account for Varioovrax's improved growth

components.name: oxygen (O2, air)
components.evidence: The Variovorax isolate grew aerobically on sugars, pyruvate and branched-chain amino acids ... predicted to encode for a highly versatile aerobic metabolism

media.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (liquid; composition as defined inline in the paper's Methods, agar omitted for liquid culture)

sources_chased.how: read corpus JATS full text; the Methods define M9 inline with NO external citation, so there is no cited recipe to chase

sources_chased.what: supplementary materials (Supplementary Information PDF; Supplementary data 1-3)
sources_chased.how: reviewed main text references to supplements; they concern genome/vitamin-pathway data, not media composition

missing_before: explicit identity of the 25% Cryptococcus spent-media supplement and its ~5 mg/L pantothenate
media.notes: Paper defines M9 inline (Methods, 'Isolate culturing and co-culturing'); there is no cited base-medium reference to expand. The stated recipe contains NO nitrogen source (no NH4Cl/ammonium), NO CaCl2, and NO trace metals or vitamin mix anywhere in the article (verified by full-text search) - therefore the ammonium, calcium, Co2+, Cu, Fe2+, Fe3+, Mn, and Zn entries in current_components are NOT supported by this paper and were dropped. Printed phosphate values (0.048/0.022 mM) are ~1000x below standard M9 and are almost certainly a units typo, but are reported verbatim per the no-fabrication rule. The two NaCl entries are listed as printed. Medium = 75% fresh M9 + 25% Cryptococcus 5-day spent-media filtrate.

media.notes: Same M9 base as the spent-media medium, here supplemented with a defined 5 mg/L pantothenate (vitamin B5) - the single tested concentration referenced in the medium id. As above, the article contains NO nitrogen source (NH4Cl/ammonium), NO CaCl2, and NO trace metals/vitamin mix (verified by full-text search), so the ammonium/calcium/Co2+/Cu/Fe/Mn/Zn entries in current_components are unsupported by this paper and were dropped. Phosphate values reported verbatim despite an apparent ~1000x units typo. No reductant/anaerobic gas - Variovorax grows aerobically.
